label: Wide-shell 1BR / 1 bath, LD style -- sibling of 1s_1br_wd_side_split_bath_gr: full-depth bedroom column on one side; the other side keeps the gr sibling's exact 2-band depth (front public band / rear bath+kitchen band), splitting the front band into living (hosts entry, left) + dining (right) side-by-side instead of one great_room. Dining stacks front-to-rear with kitchen, living stacks front-to-rear with bath -- no extra depth-band needed versus the gr sibling.

adjacencies.note: bedroom's only door -- opens east into living across the column seam, same role great played in the gr sibling.
bath sits directly beside the bedroom in the rear band -- shared wall only, no door. Unchanged from the gr sibling.
living and dining sit side-by-side in the front band -- open plan, no wall.
living (front-left-of-right-side) stacks front-to-rear with bath (rear-left-of-right-side) -- shared wall only, no door. Bath's access stays via kitchen.
dining stacks front-to-rear with kitchen -- open pass-through.
bath's only door -- opens into kitchen on the shared plumbing wall. Unchanged from the gr sibling.

notes: Sibling of 1s_1br_wd_side_split_bath_gr, created 2026-07-20 -- second attempt
at a wide LDK variant. The first attempt (living/dining STACKED as two full-
width bands in front of kitchen+bath) needed a genuinely bigger lot (true
min ~12.5x10, 51 m2) than even the round-number max (12x9). This version
avoids adding a third depth-band entirely: living and dining sit SIDE-BY-
SIDE in the same front band the gr sibling already uses, each stacking
front-to-rear with its own rear-band neighbor (living->bath, dining-
>kitchen) via match_widths, giving a clean 2x2 grid on the public side.
Layout intent (front at bottom):
Rear:  [BEDROOM (cont.) | BATH   | KITCHEN]
Front: [BEDROOM (cont.) | LIVING | DINING ]
[porch/entry]
dining stacks directly with kitchen (satisfies the default kitchen+dining-
must-stack rule) but living sits BESIDE dining, not in front of both
dining and kitchen -- requires ldk_horizontal: true. Without it, the
solver's default LDK rule (solver.py ~line 699) hard-requires
living.y_end <= dining.y (living strictly in front of dining) whenever
living_room + dining_room + kitchen all exist, which directly
contradicts a side-by-side living/dining seam -- infeasible at ANY lot
size, confirmed by probing an absurdly oversized 25x20 m lot and still
getting INFEASIBLE. Diagnosed by reading the rule in solver.py rather
than guessing from symptoms.
FEASIBILITY (2026-07-20): infeasible at min (10x8) -- min stays the gr
sibling's job by design, this topology is med/max only. Feasible from
just above 10x8 (10.25x8.2 already solves) through 11x8 (med) and 12x9
(max). living gets mechanical_vent: true -- at 12x9 specifically it came
back with 0.00 m2 window area (living's only exterior wall is the front,
and the entry door apparently ate the whole segment); same accepted
opt-out pattern kitchen already uses everywhere in this catalog.
No lot_adjustment_profiles yet -- untuned, pending real feasibility
testing.
